title: Pepper Finds the Way
body:
## Pepper Finds the Way

Pepper the cat woke up under a porch he did not know, and the wood smelled wet. Rain fell in soft drops on the leaves above him. He could not remember how he got here, but he knew this porch was not his porch.

The night before, he had chased a moth out an open window. The moth flew higher and higher across the yard. Pepper followed it across the grass, then across the next yard, then down the street. By the time he stopped chasing, he was far from his own house. He had wandered all the way to a neighborhood he did not know.

Pepper stood up and stretched. He sniffed the morning air. The rain made everything smell different. None of the smells were the smells of home.

He walked to the edge of the porch. A blue truck rolled past on the wet street. Pepper waited until it was gone. Then he stepped out into the soft rain. He shook one paw and then the other.

He walked to the corner. He stopped and listened. He sniffed again. He could hear birds and a dog barking far away. He could smell wet grass and the inside of a flowerpot. None of the sounds or smells helped him.

He turned left.

For a long time, nothing seemed right. The houses were the wrong color. The cars were the wrong shape. Then, slowly, the smells began to change. A small wind brought a smell he knew. It was the smell of his own front yard: the cedar bush by the gate.

Pepper walked faster, and he turned the second corner without slowing down. He saw the gate, and he saw his own porch with the welcome mat with the bird on it.

His person was sitting on the steps with an open umbrella. She was looking up the street. When she saw Pepper, she stood up so fast her umbrella fell to the wet grass.

She ran to him. She picked him up and put him under her chin. Pepper got her shirt wet. He did not mind. He purred all the way back to the dry house.
